chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: Which condition is necessary for a bulb to glow in a circuit?
questions.explanation: A bulb glows only when the circuit is complete and current flows.

questions.question: What will happen if a switch in a circuit is turned OFF?
questions.explanation: Turning OFF the switch breaks the circuit, stopping current flow.

questions.question: Which property of a material allows electric current to pass through it easily?
questions.explanation: Materials with high conductivity allow electric current to pass easily.

questions.question: Which effect of electric current is used in electric irons?
questions.explanation: Electric irons work by converting electrical energy into heat.

questions.question: What happens when too much current flows through a circuit?
questions.explanation: A fuse melts due to excessive heating when excess current flows.

questions.question: Which device uses the magnetic effect of electric current?
questions.explanation: Electric bells work using electromagnets created by current.

questions.question: What will happen if the number of turns in the coil of an electromagnet increases?
questions.explanation: Increasing the number of coil turns strengthens an electromagnet.

questions.question: Which core material is commonly used in electromagnets?
questions.explanation: Soft iron is used because it magnetizes easily.

questions.question: Which of the following increases the strength of an electromagnet?
questions.explanation: More current increases magnetic field strength.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of electromagnets over permanent magnets?

options.C: They can be switched on and off

questions.explanation: Electromagnets work only when current flows and can be controlled.

questions.question: Which instrument is used to detect the magnetic field around a current carrying wire?
questions.explanation: A compass needle deflects in a magnetic field.

questions.question: What type of energy conversion occurs in an electric heater?
questions.explanation: Electrical energy converts to heat energy.

questions.question: Why are wires coated with insulating materials?
questions.explanation: Insulation protects people from electric shocks.

questions.question: Which factor mainly produces heat in a wire carrying current?
questions.explanation: Heat is produced due to resistance in the conductor.

questions.question: What is the main function of a fuse in a circuit?
questions.explanation: A fuse protects appliances from excessive current.

questions.question: Which condition causes a short circuit?
questions.explanation: A short circuit occurs when wires of different potential touch.

questions.question: Which property of a heating coil prevents it from melting easily?
questions.explanation: Heating coils are made of materials with high melting points.

questions.question: What happens to the magnetic field if current direction is reversed?
questions.explanation: Reversing current changes magnetic field direction.

questions.question: Which energy transformation occurs in a glowing bulb?
questions.explanation: Bulbs convert electrical energy into light energy.

questions.question: Why are electromagnets used in cranes for lifting scrap metal?

options.B: They can be switched on and off

questions.explanation: Electromagnets can be controlled using electric current.

questions.question: Which effect of electric current produces a magnetic field around a wire?
questions.explanation: Current flowing through a conductor produces a magnetic field.

questions.question: Which component connects different parts of an electric circuit?
questions.explanation: Wires connect the different components of a circuit.

questions.question: What will happen if insulation of wires is damaged?
questions.explanation: Damaged insulation exposes wires and may cause electric shocks.

questions.question: Which factor mainly determines how strong an electromagnet becomes?
questions.explanation: More turns in the coil increase magnetic strength.

questions.question: Which device converts electrical energy into sound using electromagnets?
questions.explanation: Electric bells use electromagnets to produce sound.
